channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: RBC Capital Markets secures top analyst from Morgan Stanley to boost non-bank financials team
item.description: RBC Capital Markets is expanding its research team in the non-bank financials and insurers sectors, with the hire of Andrei Stadnik, a respected analyst previously with Morgan Stanley. Stadnik will ta...

item.title: Tributes Pour In for Former Judge President Achmat Jappie After His Passing
item.description: South Africa’s Parliament and members of the judiciary have paid tribute to retired Judge President Achmat Naeem Jappie, who passed away at the age of 70 after a short illness. Widely respected across...

item.title: Shebeshxt requires urgent leg surgery while awaiting trial
item.description: Lehlogonolo Chauke, popularly known as Shebeshxt, is due back in the Polokwane magistrate’s court for the continuation of his bail application. His lawyer, Michael Khumalo, explained that Chauke has n...

item.title: Poultry Farm Manager Kidnapped by Gunmen in Ondo State
item.description: For people wey dey Akure North, Ondo State, wahala don happen as Afolabi Ajayi, wey be manager for one poultry farm for Imafon community, don get serious gbege. Na Monday around 10:30 a.m. e reach far...

item.title: King Charles and Donald Trump have private conversation on global security during White House visit
item.description: During the first day of King Charles and Queen Camilla’s four-day visit to the United States, a lip reader has claimed to uncover a private discussion between King Charles and former President Donald ...

item.title: Explaining government-mandated subsidy charges in Philippine electricity bills
item.description: The article explains the reason behind the additional “subsidy” charges seen in electricity bills, particularly those issued by Meralco, after consumers raised concerns over rising costs. According to...

item.title: How Digital Life Shapes Parenting Challenges Today
item.description: Nowadays, digital culture is big part of daily life for both parents and children. Most families are using social media, messaging, video calls, and group chats, which influence how children form thei...

item.title: Veteran Nollywood Actor Solomon Akiyesi Dies in His Sleep
item.description: The Nigerian film industry is currently in mourning following the sudden death of veteran actor Solomon Akiyesi, who reportedly passed away on April 26, 2026. According to reports, the actor died peac...

item.title: Central forces to remain in West Bengal post-assembly elections for law and order duties
item.description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah announced that central forces will continue to stay in West Bengal for 60 more days after the assembly elections, assuring voters to cast their votes without fear. Addres...

item.title: Why Companies Benefit from Recognizing Failure Early and Redirecting Resources
item.description: The article argues that while persistence is often celebrated in business culture, it can sometimes lead companies to waste significant time and resources on failing ideas. Instead, the concept of “fa...

item.title: Train Accident Near Jakarta Claims 14 Lives, Dozens Injured
item.description: In a tragic accident near Jakarta, Indonesia, a long-distance train collided with a stationary commuter train, resulting in the death of 14 people and injuries to dozens more. The incident occurred at...

item.title: Pakistani industry voices concerns over SBP interest rate increase amid economic recovery
item.description: KARACHI: On Monday, Pakistan’s trade and industry leaders expressed strong reservations about the State Bank of Pakistan's decision to raise the interest rate by 100 basis points to 11.5 percent. They...

item.title: Oil Prices Rise Amid US-Iran Tensions Over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Oil prices have risen as President Donald Trump considers Iran’s recent proposal to end the ongoing conflict. The Iranian offer suggests the country will lift its blockade on the Strait of Hormuz, whi...

item.title: Recorded suicides linked to domestic abuse increase amid improved reporting in England and Wales
item.description: Official figures in England and Wales indicate a notable rise in the number of people who are believed to have taken their own lives following experiences of domestic abuse. According to data from the...

item.title: IAUE schedules 44th Convocation Ceremony for May 8–9, 2026
item.description: PORT HARCOURT – Ignatius Ajuru University of Education (IAUE) don announce say dem go hold their 44th convocation ceremony on May 8 and 9, 2026. The University Senate confirm the dates, and Rivers Sta...

item.title: King Charles III's US visit begins with tea and diplomacy amidst tensions
item.description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla's visit to the United States has begun under tense circumstances, with the royal couple facing a mixture of political challenges and public scrutiny. Despite the pre...

item.title: IPOA Opens 32 Positions for Kenyans Including Drivers and Office Assistants
item.description: The Independent Policing Oversight Authority (IPOA) has opened applications for 32 vacancies targeting Kenyan graduates and experienced professionals. The agency seeks to fill roles including senior a...

item.title: Gujarat local body election results: BJP leads in several wards as counting begins
item.description: Counting of votes for the 2026 Gujarat local body elections commenced on Tuesday at 9 am, covering 15 municipal corporations, 84 municipalities, 34 district panchayats, and 260 taluka panchayats. Poll...

item.title: Canadian AI Minister Arranges Meeting with Children’s Safety Advocates Following Initial Scheduling Issues
item.description: Artificial Intelligence Minister Evan Solomon of Canada has scheduled a meeting with Children First Canada, a national charity advocating for enhanced online safety for children. The meeting follows e...

item.title: Declining snow levels in HKH region raise serious water security concerns for Indus Basin
item.description: A recent report by the International Centre for Integrated Mountain Development (ICIMOD) highlights a worrying and continued decline in snow cover across the Hindu Kush-Himalaya (HKH) region, which di...
